i kind of wanted to re-bring this point up that was apparently here earlier
so the classical latin in this translation that was chosen is really poor in general, but i'm not particularly going to criticize that in depth, that feels a bit unfair all things considered
but surely the whole point of juliette's title was to compare her to Lucifer in a religious and mythological sense
since the (well-known) latin word for lightbearer or lightbringer is just Lucifer

this was in response to niko wondering why pontos didn't age, and my theory on it
.
.
.
||because "aging" as a concept (time passing, people's memories) is why Leisha & Jago chose to go back/leave Ebwynnos, he chose to show his "physical age" as an old man to prove that "age" or "time" has no effect on him, that despite being "old" and having lived a "long life" like they did, he still will always return to eternity/Ebwynnos

which makes it so much more messed up how his viewpoint is, if you consider his actions during the deepdive towards the keeper-as-jago. 

he's running, first from having a proper conversation with Leisha at the beginning of the deepdive, when he pretends to be asleep w/ jago, and then later when they sneak out. he then later "runs" from emotional/close connections through his actions w/ his admirers, choosing to keep them at a distance, not accepting directly or answering their gifts, instead leaving the burden of response to Leisha. But the thing is, overall, even if it seems like he wants to go and fight w/ the gaunts against the cultists, he runs at the end, not wanting to face reality. 

He creates Ebwynnos, his "eternity" where everyone is alive and free to live forever, but in reality it's all in his mind, just escaping the fact that Jago and Leisha are dead, that everyone he knew is gone. pontos doesn't want to confront it, and while he knows that morally, it's the wrong thing to do, to continue clinging onto his memories, he continues to do so, even forcing Leisha & Jago to stay against their will, solely because he's a sad pile who refuses to man up and face the fact that he should get a life||

Btw , out of curiosity , what's ossdd-1 system?
And before you ask I read everyone's bio

quickest explanation is: you know how DID is various alters, with amnesia between them, so one alter wouldn't remember what another alter did when in front? OSDD-1 is like that, but we don't have that amnesia
(we have a level of *emotional* amnesia in that the feelings of one alter don't carry over to the next when one of us swaps front, but not the same way DID does)

that's all we'll say on it bc we are Not a medical professional and are just going off our own experience with it 👍 
(anyways 24 is a good system rep and we'll die on that hill, relatedly)
